notes: task-estimate-marker for kiro-cli. The agent emits its own progress estimate in-band as a hidden HTML comment in assistant text — <!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} --> — advanced 0->1->2->3 across four working turns (CONFIRMED live: the marker survived verbatim into the <uuid>.jsonl on all four AssistantMessage text blocks at content[].data, scanned by parser.go:62-68 via tailer.ScanTaskEstimate on the FULL block before the 200-rune display truncation), and irrlicht surfaces task_estimate {total_rounds:3, completed_rounds:M, updated_at} + a monotone-non-increasing task_completion_eta on the session metrics WHILE working, then drops both fields at ready. The downstream pipeline (tailer persistence -> domain TaskEstimate/TaskCompletionEta projection at elapsed/completed -> API metrics, suppressed off the working state) is shared with claudecode/aider. The marker rule is delivered IN the prompt (no instruction file is seeded) — mirroring the aider cell — so there is no seed_instruction driver gap. EMISSION SETTLED (was provisional): the live record run (recordings/2026-06-05-03-29-30_irrlichd-0.4.8+cdefa05.dirty) confirmed kiro's model re-emits the marker turn-over-turn with climbing completed_rounds. SESSION IDENTITY (kiro-specific, BACKFLOW-CORRECTED 2026-06-05): the live recording refuted the earlier two-step presession-proc-<PID> -> new-UUID-reconcile hypothesis. In this run kiro minted the UUID session DIRECTLY in ready (transcript_new -> state_transition ready 'new session created' on the <uuid> session; the transcript landed before the daemon registered any presession row), then bound the PID via DiscoverPIDByCWD — the same single-session-birth convention as the kiro basic-turn / self-correction cells. The expected.jsonl phase model was corrected to a single session_birth (ready, rel=start) + pid_bind + four working/ready round turns + settle + teardown, all on the one UUID session. OBSERVATIONS: the kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar holds credits + model_id, input/output_token_count=0; the parser does not read it), so the observations vector asserts model unknown (omitted), tokens_nonzero=false, cost_nonzero=false — the rate/ETA assertions are still observable because they derive from the marker + the daemon's own elapsed wall-clock, not agent-reported tokens. This expected.jsonl is the documented target the recording validates against.

text: The kiro UUID session is minted DIRECTLY in ready (transcript_new -> state_transition ready 'new session created' on the <uuid> session). kiro writes its <uuid>.jsonl lazily on first prompt; in this run the transcript landed before any presession proc-<PID> row, so the UUID session IS the birth (basic-turn / self-correction convention).
PID bound to the UUID session (DiscoverPIDByCWD on the kiro-cli chat parent's cwd).

Round-1 prompt -> working. The agent emits the first marker (completed_rounds:0/total_rounds:3); EXPECTED: task_estimate.total_rounds=3, task_estimate.completed_rounds=0 present on the working session's metrics.

Round-1 turn settles to ready (text-only assistant message = turn_done). The session is no longer working, so task_estimate/task_completion_eta are suppressed here (client gate) — they reappear on the next working turn.

Round-2 prompt -> working; marker advances to completed_rounds:1. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=1 (<= total_rounds, non-decreasing vs round 1), updated_at advances, and task_completion_eta is present and <= its round-1 value (ETA burns down).
Round-2 turn settles to ready; task_estimate/task_completion_eta suppressed at ready (client gate).

Round-3 prompt -> working; marker advances to completed_rounds:2. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=2 (non-decreasing), updated_at advances, task_completion_eta <= the round-2 value (continued burndown).
Round-3 turn settles to ready; fields suppressed at ready (client gate).
Final working turn -> marker reaches completed_rounds:3 == total_rounds (the agent reports the task done). EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=3=total_rounds on the working metrics.
After a >=10s trailing idle the session settles to ready. EXPECTED: task_estimate AND task_completion_eta are ABSENT once the session is no longer working (suppressed-by-state, client gate).
TUI torn down once after the final settle -> a process_exited on the UUID session; state immediately before teardown is ready. Final state: ready.
